threat_model: A runtime-promotion approval packet could be mistaken for runtime promotion authorization if it permits authority flags, promotion-ready scenario claims, or closure claims. The mitigation is a schema-backed local-only packet with hard false authority fields, remaining AwaitingEvidence replay/recovery and terminal closure gates, and focused tests for authority, gate, criteria, and source-receipt drift.

required_checks.check: authority boundary check proves the approval packet keeps runtime promotion, execution, dispatch, runtime replanning, success claims, and terminal closure false
promotion gate check proves replay/recovery and terminal closure gates remain required after approval evidence is collected
packet integrity check proves the checked-in fixture matches the deterministic produced packet and validates against the schema
audit visibility check records passed approval criteria and one no-effect scenario approval for every expected plan class

findings.mitigation: The schema and validator hard-deny runtime_promotion_authorized, execution_allowed, dispatch_allowed, runtime_replanning_enabled, success_claim_allowed, and terminal_closure.
The packet requires replay/recovery and terminal closure gates to remain AwaitingEvidence after runtime-promotion approval evidence is collected.

mitigations: keep the approval packet local, deterministic, and read-only
hard-deny all runtime promotion and terminal closure fields in the schema
bind the packet to the deterministic observation receipt id and hash
record passed approval criteria without claiming runtime promotion readiness
